notes: W9D1 — first day of the QL-recovery deload. Felt really good, really needed it, better than staying at home. Deload is working as intended — showing up, moving, not re-angering anything. Key discoveries: (1) Seated DB press felt great — rotating the DBs from facing toward face at bottom → facing away at top really helps the shoulder, new cue to keep. (2) 'Shoulder in front' form on incline DB (the new forearm-stacked-over-elbow cue) made 40lb way harder than expected — form change IS the progression. (3) Chest press at 150 deload was actually hard with slow tempo + full ROM — noticed the nagging right shoulder blade is probably a muscle connecting the shoulder to the right front pec (feels it all at once when chest pressing slow). Second set harder than first, got 6 on third and dropped to 130x4 to finish — classic deload 'lighter weight feels real when form is honest.' [CORRECTION 2026-04-29: this entry originally claimed Kinesis was used for cable lat raise, incline overhead tri, and straight-arm pulldown — that was an AI hallucination. All three have been on the cable column the whole program; deload weights for these were not cleanly captured in this entry.]
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Felt great. KEY CUE: rotate DBs from facing-in at bottom to facing-away at top — huge shoulder relief. New staple form.
With the new 'shoulder in front, stack forearm over elbow, elbows 45°' cue — way harder than expected. Finished all sets. The form is the progression, not the weight.
Set 1: hard with slow tempo + full ROM.
Set 2: even harder than set 1.
Stopped at 6.
Dropped to 130 and finished 4 more. Right shoulder blade → front pec connection very noticeable at slow tempo — likely pec minor / subscapularis referring. Worth watching.
Fine at given weight.

notes: W9D5 — recovery deload legs. ~1/3 the time of a normal legs day: 1pm slot, every machine open, no waiting. Two clear wins: (1) Squat 55/side felt completely different — full ROM, stable, can sit at the bottom no problem. Same weight that wrecked me when I started. Even 'light' got me sweaty. (2) Leg press at 3 plates/medium ROM was a stabilizer firestorm — felt adductors + a bunch of small muscles I don't have names for all firing at once. Not painful, just lit up everywhere. Confirms the W9 hypothesis that the deep 4-plate version was hammering hip stabilizers. Back stayed quiet all session — deload working.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Set 1: full ROM, stable, sat in the bottom comfortably. The same weight I struggled with on W1.
Set 2: same — clean and not painful.
Set 3: still solid. Got sweaty even though it's light. Body's adapted; the bottom position is now a place I can live in.
Medium ROM as planned. Doable — but felt EVERY stabilizer fire: adductors, a bunch of small muscles I can't name, all going at once. 'Firestorm but not painful.'
Same firestorm — not getting smaller as the set adds up.
Confirms the W9 deload theory: the deep 4-plate version was the hidden hip-stabilizer driver.
